chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1301 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.7105/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.7106/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 20.05.2025 M/s Imran Ali Mithani & Mohsin Ali, advocates for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicants/accused Muhammad Rafiq Haslo son of Bakhshal alias Bux Ali and Faraz Hussain son of Muhammad Rafiq Haslo seek protective bail in FIR No.37/2025, registered at P.S. Waleed, District Larkana, for offence under sections 506-B, 504, 337-H(ii), 34, PPC. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicants is innocent and they have been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives otherwise he has nothing to do with the alleged offence; that no specific role has been assigned to the applicants/accused in the FIR; that they are law abiding citizens and intend to surrender before learned trial Court concerned but they apprehend their arrest at the hands of local police in order to humiliate and disgrace them, as such, they need some protection to enable to appear before the trial Court concerned. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named for a period of seven days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named for a period of seven days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever applicants surrender before the trial Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicants fail to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, their bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
